articles.title: Dolby SVP Shriram Revankar sells $164,700 in company stock
articles.summary: Shriram Revankar, Senior Vice President at Dolby Laboratories, sold 3,000 shares of company stock for $164,700 on May 15, 2026, executed via a pre-arranged trading plan. This sale occurred while the stock was near its 52-week low, despite recent positive Q2 2026 earnings where Dolby surpassed EPS and revenue forecasts. The company also disposed of shares to cover withholding taxes, and Revankar now holds 76,274 shares including restricted stock units.
articles.content: Shriram Revankar, Senior Vice President at Dolby Laboratories, sold 3,000 shares of company stock for $164,700 on May 15, 2026, executed via a pre-arranged trading plan. articles.title: Analog Devices Earnings: Raising Fair Value Estimate Thanks to AI Boom
articles.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) reported strong fiscal second-quarter results with revenue up 37% year over year, exceeding guidance. The company is benefiting significantly from the AI infrastructure buildout and cyclical recovery in core product lines, leading Morningstar to raise its fair value estimate to $380 per share. ADI also announced the acquisition of Empower Semiconductor for $1.5 billion, a strategic move to bolster its position in power management semiconductors for AI servers.
articles.content: Analog Devices (ADI) reported strong fiscal second-quarter results with revenue up 37% year over year, exceeding guidance. The company is benefiting significantly from the AI infrastructure buildout and cyclical recovery in core product lines, leading Morningstar to raise its fair value estimate to $380 per share. ADI also announced the acquisition of Empower Semiconductor for $1.5 billion, a strategic move to bolster its position in power management semiconductors for AI servers.

articles.title: This AI drone app just won Verizon’s $25,000 prize for first responders
articles.summary: Verizon has named Rian Karim of San Jose the winner of its first Verizon Frontline App Developer Challenge, awarding a $25,000 prize for his AI-powered drone app, Apex Eye. This app transforms drone video and data into clear operational pictures for first responders. All three finalists, including those who developed a clinical decision support tool for paramedics and a real-time situational awareness app, will join the Verizon Frontline Innovation Program to further develop their solutions.
articles.content: Verizon has named Rian Karim of San Jose the winner of its first Verizon Frontline App Developer Challenge, awarding a $25,000 prize for his AI-powered drone app, Apex Eye. This app transforms drone video and data into clear operational pictures for first responders. All three finalists, including those who developed a clinical decision support tool for paramedics and a real-time situational awareness app, will join the Verizon Frontline Innovation Program to further develop their solutions.

articles.title: Asian Community Development Council Joins the Sands Cares Accelerator
articles.summary: The Asian Community Development Council (ACDC) has joined the Sands Cares Accelerator, a three-year program by Las Vegas Sands (NYSE: LVS) aimed at helping non-profits increase their community impact. ACDC plans to use this membership to establish a permanent cultural and resource hub for the Asian American Native Hawaiian Pacific Islander (AANHPI) community in Las Vegas. Sands is supporting ACDC with a $150,000 donation for its API Language Link and youth development programs, continuing past support for various ACDC initiatives.
articles.content: The Asian Community Development Council (ACDC) has joined the Sands Cares Accelerator, a three-year program by Las Vegas Sands (NYSE: LVS) aimed at helping non-profits increase their community impact. ACDC plans to use this membership to establish a permanent cultural and resource hub for the Asian American Native Hawaiian Pacific Islander (AANHPI) community in Las Vegas. Sands is supporting ACDC with a $150,000 donation for its API Language Link and youth development programs, continuing past support for various ACDC initiatives.
